Title: Store and organization both have whitelabeling settings - organization branding overrides store
Preconditions: Store has White Labeling with store-level logo/favicon/theme; Organization has custom logo/favicon/theme configured
Test_Data: admin={{BACK_URL}}, storefront={{FRONT_URL}}, admin_user={{ADMIN_EMAIL}}, admin_password={{ADMIN_PASSWORD}}, org_user={{ORG_USER_EMAIL}}, org_password={{ORG_USER_PASSWORD}}
Steps:
[NAV] 1. Navigate to Admin > Stores > B2B-store
[ACT] 2. Open White Labeling settings
[ACT] 3. Upload store logo: store-logo.png
[ACT] 4. Set theme: Default
[ACT] 5. Save
[NAV] 6. Navigate to Customer > Organizations > Branded Org
[ACT] 7. Open White Labeling settings
[ASSERT] 8. Verify Enabled = ON
[ACT] 9. Upload organization logo: org-logo.png
[ACT] 10. Upload organization favicon: org-favicon.ico
[ACT] 11. Set theme preset: Coffee
[ACT] 12. Configure footer links list: org-footer-links
[ACT] 13. Configure main menu links list: org-main-menu
[ACT] 14. Save organization settings
[NAV] 15. Sign in to storefront with user from Branded Org
[NAV] 16. Navigate to homepage
[ASSERT] 17. Inspect header logo (should show org logo, not store logo)
[ASSERT] 18. Inspect favicon (should show org favicon)
[ASSERT] 19. Inspect theme (should be Coffee, not Default)
[ASSERT] 20. Inspect main menu links structure
[ASSERT] 21. Inspect footer links structure
Assertions:
[DOM] Organization logo displayed in header (org-logo.png, NOT store-logo.png)
[DOM] Organization favicon displayed in browser tab
[STATE] Coffee theme applied (NOT Default)
[DOM] Org-specific main menu links visible
[DOM] Org-specific footer links visible
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
[NETWORK] org logo image loads with HTTP 200
Failure_Signals: Store logo shown instead of org logo; Store theme applied instead of org theme; Org footer/menu links missing
Cleanup: restore org settings to pre-test state; restore store settings

Title: Set non-existent theme preset - default theme applied as fallback
Preconditions: Store/Organization White Labeling configured; Access to backend
Test_Data: admin={{BACK_URL}}, storefront={{FRONT_URL}}, admin_user={{ADMIN_EMAIL}}, admin_password={{ADMIN_PASSWORD}}, org_user={{ORG_USER_EMAIL}}, org_password={{ORG_USER_PASSWORD}}
Steps:
[NAV] 1. Navigate to Admin > Customer > Organizations > Test Org
[ACT] 2. Open White Labeling settings
[ACT] 3. Directly set themePresetName field to non-existent value: Mercury (outdated preset)
[ACT] 4. Save settings
[NAV] 5. Sign in to storefront with user from Test Org
[NAV] 6. Navigate to homepage
[ASSERT] 7. Inspect page theme colors and styles
[ASSERT] 8. Verify theme applied
Assertions:
[STATE] Default theme preset applied as fallback (NOT Mercury)
[DOM] Page renders correctly with default colors
[DOM] No broken or missing styles visible
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] no theme-related JS errors or warnings
[NETWORK] theme CSS loads with HTTP 200
Failure_Signals: Mercury theme applied (should not exist); Console errors on theme load; Broken styles or missing CSS
Cleanup: restore org themePresetName to valid preset

Title: Set custom logo and favicon for organization - displayed after sign-in
Preconditions: Organization exists: Company1; White Labeling enabled; Custom logo and favicon assets prepared
Test_Data: admin={{BACK_URL}}, storefront={{FRONT_URL}}, admin_user={{ADMIN_EMAIL}}, admin_password={{ADMIN_PASSWORD}}, org_user={{ORG_USER_EMAIL}}, org_password={{ORG_USER_PASSWORD}}
Steps:
[NAV] 1. Navigate to Admin > Customer > Organizations > Company1
[ACT] 2. Open White Labeling settings
[ASSERT] 3. Verify Enabled = ON
[ACT] 4. Upload custom logo: company1-logo.png (PNG, 200x60px)
[ACT] 5. Upload custom favicon: company1-favicon.ico (ICO, 32x32px)
[ACT] 6. Save settings
[NAV] 7. Open new browser tab
[NAV] 8. Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}
[ACT] 9. Sign in with user account from Company1 organization
[WAIT] 10. After successful sign-in, wait for redirect to homepage
[ASSERT] 11. Inspect header
[ASSERT] 12. Inspect browser tab favicon
Assertions:
[DOM] company1-logo.png visible in header
[DOM] company1-favicon.ico displayed in browser tab
[DOM] Logo is properly sized (not distorted)
[STATE] Logo links to homepage when clicked
[DOM] Branding persists across catalog, product, cart pages
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
[NETWORK] company1-logo.png loads with HTTP 200
Failure_Signals: Default logo shown instead of company1 logo; Broken image in header; Favicon not updated

Test_Data: admin={{BACK_URL}}, storefront={{FRONT_URL}}, admin_user={{ADMIN_EMAIL}}, admin_password={{ADMIN_PASSWORD}}, org_user={{ORG_USER_EMAIL}}, org_password={{ORG_USER_PASSWORD}}

[ACT] 2. Open White Labeling settings
[ASSERT] 3. Verify custom logo and favicon are uploaded
[ACT] 4. Set Enabled = OFF
[ACT] 5. Save settings
[NAV] 6. Sign in to storefront with Company1 user
[NAV] 7. Navigate to homepage
[ASSERT] 8. Inspect header logo
[ASSERT] 9. Inspect browser tab favicon

[ASSERT] 11. Verify link colors

Title: Switch organization - verify branding updates dynamically (logo and favicon)
Preconditions: User account is member of 2 organizations: Org A (logo-a.png, favicon-a.ico, Default theme) and Org B (logo-b.png, favicon-b.ico, Coffee theme)
Steps:
[NAV] 1. Sign in to storefront with multi-org user account
[ASSERT] 2. Verify current organization is Org A
[ASSERT] 3. Inspect header logo (should be logo-a.png)
[ASSERT] 4. Inspect browser tab favicon (should be favicon-a.ico)
[ASSERT] 5. Inspect theme colors (should be Default)
[ACT] 6. Click organization switcher in header
[ACT] 7. Select Org B from dropdown
[WAIT] 8. Wait for organization switch to complete
[ASSERT] 9. Verify page refreshes or updates
[ASSERT] 10. Inspect header logo (should NOW be logo-b.png)
[ASSERT] 11. Inspect browser tab favicon (should NOW be favicon-b.ico)
[ASSERT] 12. Inspect theme colors (should NOW be Coffee)
[ACT] 13. Switch back to Org A
[ASSERT] 14. Verify logo/favicon/theme revert to Org A branding
Assertions:
[DOM] Switching Org A to Org B updates header logo from logo-a.png to logo-b.png
[DOM] Browser tab favicon updates from favicon-a.ico to favicon-b.ico
[STATE] Theme updates from Default to Coffee
[STATE] Switching back to Org A restores Org A branding
[STATE] All branding changes persist across page navigation after switch
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
[NETWORK] new logo image loads with HTTP 200 after switch
Failure_Signals: Old org logo still shown after switch; Stale branding from previous org; Theme not updating; Flash of wrong branding during transition

Title: Switch organization - verify main menu links update dynamically
Preconditions: User is member of Org A (main menu: Products, Services, Contact) and Org B (main menu: Solutions, Partners, Support, Blog)
Steps:
[NAV] 1. Sign in with multi-org user
[ASSERT] 2. Verify current org is Org A
[ASSERT] 3. Inspect main menu links in header: Products, Services, Contact
[ACT] 4. Hover over links to verify dropdowns (if any)
[ACT] 5. Switch organization to Org B
[WAIT] 6. Wait for refresh
[ASSERT] 7. Inspect main menu links in header: should NOW show Solutions, Partners, Support, Blog
[ASSERT] 8. Verify main menu structure changed
[ACT] 9. Switch back to Org A
[ASSERT] 10. Verify main menu reverts to Products, Services, Contact
Assertions:
[DOM] Org A displays main menu: Products, Services, Contact
[DOM] Switching to Org B updates main menu to: Solutions, Partners, Support, Blog
[DOM] Switching back to Org A restores original main menu
[STATE] No stale links from previous org displayed
[NAV] Navigation works correctly after org switch
Failure_Signals: Stale main menu links from previous org; Menu not updating after switch; Navigation broken after switch

Title: Switch organization - verify footer links update dynamically
Preconditions: User is member of Org A (footer: About, Support, Legal) and Org B (footer: Company, Help, Policies, Careers)
Steps:
[NAV] 1. Sign in with multi-org user (Org A)
[SCROLL] 2. Scroll to footer
[ASSERT] 3. Verify footer links: About, Support, Legal
[ACT] 4. Switch organization to Org B
[WAIT] 5. Wait for refresh
[SCROLL] 6. Scroll to footer
[ASSERT] 7. Verify footer links updated: Company, Help, Policies, Careers
[ACT] 8. Switch back to Org A
[ASSERT] 9. Verify footer reverts
Assertions:
[DOM] Org A displays footer: About, Support, Legal
[DOM] Switching to Org B updates footer to: Company, Help, Policies, Careers
[STATE] Switching back restores Org A footer
[STATE] No stale footer links displayed
Failure_Signals: Stale footer links from previous org; Footer not updating after switch; Wrong hierarchy displayed

Title: Switch organization - verify theme preset changes dynamically
Preconditions: User is member of Org A (Default theme) and Org B (Coffee theme)
Steps:
[NAV] 1. Sign in with multi-org user (Org A with Default theme)
[ASSERT] 2. Inspect page CSS variables and colors
[ASSERT] 3. Note Default theme colors (header, buttons, links)
[ACT] 4. Switch organization to Org B (Coffee theme)
[WAIT] 5. Wait for refresh
[ASSERT] 6. Inspect CSS variables again
[ASSERT] 7. Verify colors changed to Coffee theme
[ASSERT] 8. Verify visual difference (browns/creams instead of default blue/gray)
[ACT] 9. Switch back to Org A
[ASSERT] 10. Verify theme reverts to Default
Assertions:
[FORMAT] Org A displays Default theme colors
[FORMAT] Switching to Org B applies Coffee theme (visual color change obvious)
[FORMAT] CSS variables update to Coffee theme values
[FORMAT] Switching back to Org A restores Default theme
[STATE] Theme transition is smooth (no flicker or broken styles)
Failure_Signals: Theme not changing after org switch; Stale theme from previous org; CSS variables not updating; Theme flicker during transition

Title: Verify branding transition is smooth during organization switch (no flash of wrong branding)
Preconditions: User is member of 2 organizations with distinct branding; Fast internet connection for accurate performance test
Steps:
[NAV] 1. Sign in with multi-org user (Org A)
[ACT] 2. Open browser DevTools > Network tab
[ACT] 3. Switch organization to Org B
[ASSERT] 4. Observe page reload/refresh
[ASSERT] 5. Watch for logo/favicon loading
[ASSERT] 6. Verify no flash of old branding during transition
[ASSERT] 7. Measure time for branding to fully update
[ACT] 8. Repeat switch 3 times (Org A to Org B and back)
Assertions:
[STATE] No flash of Org A logo before Org B logo loads
[STATE] No flash of Org A favicon before Org B favicon loads
[STATE] Theme colors transition smoothly (no jarring color flash)
[FORMAT] Branding update completes within 1-2 seconds
[STATE] No broken images or layout during transition
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
[NETWORK] new branding assets load with HTTP 200 within 2 seconds
Failure_Signals: Flash of wrong branding during org switch; Broken images during transition; Transition takes more than 2 seconds; Layout breaks during switch

Title: Verify logo upload validation - accepted file formats (PNG SVG JPEG)
Preconditions: Admin access to White Labeling settings; Test image files: logo.png logo.svg logo.jpg
Steps:
[NAV] 1. Navigate to Admin > Organizations > Test Org
[ACT] 2. Open White Labeling settings
[ACT] 3. Test 1: Upload logo.png (valid PNG file)
[ASSERT] 4. Verify upload succeeds
[ACT] 5. Save settings
[ACT] 6. Test 2: Upload logo.svg (valid SVG file)
[ASSERT] 7. Verify upload succeeds
[ACT] 8. Save settings
[ACT] 9. Test 3: Upload logo.jpg (valid JPEG file)
[ASSERT] 10. Verify upload succeeds
[ACT] 11. Save settings
[ASSERT] 12. For each upload, verify logo displays correctly on storefront
Assertions:
[STATE] PNG upload succeeds without error
[STATE] SVG upload succeeds without error
[STATE] JPEG upload succeeds without error
[DOM] Uploaded logos display correctly on storefront
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[ADMIN] settings saved successfully for each format
[CONSOLE] no file upload errors
Failure_Signals: Upload rejected for valid PNG/SVG/JPEG; Error message shown for valid formats; Logo not displaying on storefront after upload
Cleanup: remove uploaded logos and restore original

Title: Verify logo upload validation - rejected file formats (GIF BMP TXT)
Preconditions: Admin access to White Labeling settings; Test invalid files: logo.gif logo.bmp logo.txt
Steps:
[NAV] 1. Navigate to Admin > Organizations > Test Org
[ACT] 2. Open White Labeling settings
[ACT] 3. Test 1: Attempt to upload logo.gif (invalid format)
[ASSERT] 4. Verify upload is REJECTED with validation error
[ACT] 5. Test 2: Attempt to upload logo.bmp (invalid format)
[ASSERT] 6. Verify upload is REJECTED
[ACT] 7. Test 3: Attempt to upload logo.txt (invalid format)
[ASSERT] 8. Verify upload is REJECTED
[ASSERT] 9. Verify error messages are clear: e.g. "Only PNG, SVG, and JPEG formats are allowed"
[ASSERT] 10. Verify settings do NOT save with invalid file
Assertions:
[STATE] GIF upload is rejected with error message
[STATE] BMP upload is rejected with error message
[STATE] TXT upload is rejected with error message
[DOM] Error messages are clear and user-friendly
[STATE] Invalid file is NOT uploaded to server
[STATE] Logo field remains empty or retains previous valid logo
Failure_Signals: Invalid formats accepted without error; No error message shown; Invalid file saved to server

Title: Verify logo upload validation - file size limit (max 2MB)
Preconditions: Admin access; Test large logo file: logo-large.png (3MB or larger exceeds limit)
Steps:
[NAV] 1. Navigate to Admin > Organizations > Test Org
[ACT] 2. Open White Labeling settings
[ACT] 3. Attempt to upload logo-large.png (3MB file size)
[ASSERT] 4. Verify upload is REJECTED due to file size
[ASSERT] 5. Verify error message: "File size exceeds 2MB limit" or similar
[ACT] 6. Attempt to upload valid-sized logo (< 2MB)
[ASSERT] 7. Verify upload succeeds
Assertions:
[STATE] Files larger than 2MB are rejected
[DOM] Error message clearly states file size limit
[STATE] User is NOT able to save with oversized file
[STATE] Valid-sized files (< 2MB) are accepted
Failure_Signals: Oversized file accepted without error; No error message for size limit; Valid file rejected as too large

Title: Verify favicon upload validation - accepted file format (ICO PNG)
Preconditions: Admin access; Test favicon files: favicon.ico favicon.png
Steps:
[NAV] 1. Navigate to Admin > Organizations > Test Org
[ACT] 2. Open White Labeling settings
[ACT] 3. Test 1: Upload favicon.ico (valid ICO file, 32x32)
[ASSERT] 4. Verify upload succeeds
[ACT] 5. Save settings
[ASSERT] 6. Verify favicon displays in storefront browser tab
[ACT] 7. Test 2: Upload favicon.png (valid PNG file, 32x32)
[ASSERT] 8. Verify upload succeeds
[ASSERT] 9. Verify PNG favicon displays correctly
Assertions:
[STATE] ICO format accepted and uploads successfully
[STATE] PNG format accepted and uploads successfully
[DOM] Both formats display correctly in browser tab
[DOM] Favicon is properly generated in multiple sizes (16x16, 32x32, 180x180) if thumbnail job runs
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] no file upload errors
[NETWORK] favicon loads with HTTP 200 in browser tab
Failure_Signals: ICO format rejected; PNG format rejected; Favicon not showing in browser tab after upload
Cleanup: remove uploaded favicons and restore original
